messages.content: please rephrase this paragraph 'Rosmarinus officinalis has long been used as a medicinal plant. In this context, a plethora of biological effects and health benefits have been attributed to rosemary essential oil, leaf extracts, and isolated substances, including antibacterial '

Rosemary essential oil, leaf extracts, and isolated substances have been credited with a variety of biological effects and health advantages, including antibacterial properties, due to the extensive use of Rosmarinus officinalis as a medicinal plant.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Phytochemical analyses of rosemary extracts and essential oils have revealed several substances with recognized antioxidant properties, such as flavonoids and phenolic compounds Dorta. Such antioxidant activities of rosmarinus officinalis might help to explain some of its biological effects. ’

Upon conducting phytochemical analyses on rosemary extracts and essential oils, it has been identified that various substances, including flavonoids and phenolic compounds Dorta, possess established antioxidant properties. These antioxidant activities of rosmarinus officinalis may contribute to elucidating certain biological effects associated with it.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Cinnamon and rosemary administration or both to nephrotoxic rats led to a significant decrease in the mean value off MDA with significant increase of renal GSH. The current understanding of acrylamide toxic effects on the renal tissue of animals has been investigated, the present study was aimed to assess the ameliorative effects of cinnamon and rosemary against acrylamide induced nephrotoxicity in adult male albino rats.. ’

The present study aimed to investigate the ameliorative effects of cinnamon and rosemary on acrylamide-induced nephrotoxicity in adult male albino rats. Administration of either cinnamon or rosemary, or both, to nephrotoxic rats resulted in a significant reduction in the average value of MDA (malondialdehyde) along with a significant increase in renal GSH (glutathione). This study contributes to the current understanding of the toxic effects of acrylamide on renal tissue in animals.
please rephrase this paragraph 'Cinnamon and rosemary administration or both to nephrotoxic rats led to a significant decrease in the mean value off MDA with significant increase of renal GSH.’

The administration of cinnamon, rosemary, or a combination of both to nephrotoxic rats resulted in a notable reduction in the average value of MDA (malondialdehyde), accompanied by a significant increase in renal GSH (glutathione) levels.

please rephrase this paragraph 'The current understanding of acrylamide toxic effects on the renal tissue of animals has been investigated, the present study was aimed to assess the ameliorative effects of cinnamon and rosemary against acrylamide induced nephrotoxicity in adult male albino rats.’

In light of the ongoing research into the toxic effects of acrylamide on animal renal tissue, the primary objective of this study was to evaluate the potential ameliorative effects of cinnamon and rosemary in mitigating acrylamide-induced nephrotoxicity in adult male albino rats.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Acclimatized rats were divided into seven groups; each consists of 10 rats. To assess the aim of the present work groups were treated differently as follow:
	Group1: Rats which served as control group and were administered distilled water orally once daily for 28 consecutive days. 
	Group 2: Rats were subjected to induction of renal injury by administering acrylamide in a dose of (20 mg/kg. b. wt) orally once daily for 28 consecutive days.
	Group 3: Rats in this group were administered cinnamon oil in a dose of (200 mg/kg.b. wt) orally once daily for 28 consecutive days.
	Group 4: Rats in this group were administered rosemary oil in a dose of (250 mg/kg.b. wt) orally once daily for 28 consecutive days.
	Group 5: Rats in this group were administered acrylamide (20mg/kg b. wt) followed by cinnamon oil (200 mg/kg b. wt) once daily for 28 consecutive days.
	Group 6: Rats in this group were administered acrylamide (20mg/kg b. wt) followed by rosemary oil (250 mg/kg b. wt) once daily for 28 consecutive days.
	Group 7: Rats in this group were administered acrylamide (20mg/kg b. wt) followed by cinnamon oil (200 mg/kg b. wt) then rosemary oil (250 mg/kg b. wt) once daily for 28 consecutive days.’

Acclimatized rats were distributed into seven groups, each comprising 10 rats, to fulfill the objectives of the present study. The treatment of each group was as follows:
- Group 1: Rats in this control group were orally administered distilled water once daily for 28 consecutive days.
- Group 2: Rats in this group were induced with renal injury by orally administering acrylamide at a dose of 20 mg/kg body weight once daily for 28 consecutive days.
- Group 3: Rats in this group received oral administration of cinnamon oil at a dose of 200 mg/kg body weight once daily for 28 consecutive days.
- Group 4: Rats in this group were orally administered rosemary oil at a dose of 250 mg/kg body weight once daily for 28 consecutive days.
- Group 5: Rats in this group received acrylamide at a dose of 20 mg/kg body weight followed by oral administration of cinnamon oil at a dose of 200 mg/kg body weight once daily for 28 consecutive days.
- Group 6: Rats in this group received acrylamide at a dose of 20 mg/kg body weight followed by oral administration of rosemary oil at a dose of 250 mg/kg body weight once daily for 28 consecutive days.
- Group 7: Rats in this group received acrylamide at a dose of 20 mg/kg body weight followed by oral administration of cinnamon oil at a dose of 200 mg/kg body weight, and then rosemary oil at a dose of 250 mg/kg body weight once daily for 28 consecutive days.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Acrylamide: It was obtained as white crystalline powder from Fine –Chem Limited Company. The purity of the powder was about 98.5%.the powder was freshly dissolved in distilled water just before administration. It was used at a dose of 20 mg/kg. b. wt.  
Cinnamon and rosemary oils: They were obtained from El-captain Company for extracting natural oils, herbs and cosmetics, El Obour City, Cairo, Egypt. They were used at a dose of 200 mg/kg.b. wt for cinnamon (Prajapati et al., 2019) and 250 mg/kg.b. wt for rosemary respectively.
Kits: The diagnostic kits for estimating urea and creatinine, the diagnostic kits of estimating oxidative stress markers, Malondialdehyde (MDA), Glutathione (GSH) and Catalase (CAT) in renal tissue homogenate were all obtained from BioDiagnostic Company, Dokki, Giza, Egypt.’

Acrylamide: Fine-Chem Limited Company provided the white crystalline powder form of acrylamide, with a powder purity of approximately 98.5%. Prior to administration, the powder was freshly dissolved in distilled water. Acrylamide was utilized at a dose of 20 mg/kg body weight.

Cinnamon and rosemary oils: El-captain Company, based in El Obour City, Cairo, Egypt, supplied the cinnamon and rosemary oils used in the study. Cinnamon oil was employed at a dose of 200 mg/kg body weight (Prajapati et al., 2019), while rosemary oil was utilized at a dose of 250 mg/kg body weight.

Kits: BioDiagnostic Company, situated in Dokki, Giza, Egypt, provided the diagnostic kits required for estimating markers such as urea and creatinine, as well as oxidative stress markers including Malondialdehyde (MDA), Glutathione (GSH), and Catalase (CAT) in renal tissue homogenate.

please rephrase this paragraph 'The present study was carried out on total number of 70 male white albino Wister rats weighting 180-200 gm. Rats were obtained from Center of laboratory animals, Faculty of Veterinary Medicine, Benha University, Egypt. Rats were acclimatized for one week prior to the experiment. All rats received standard laboratory balanced commercial diet and water ad libitum. The research was conducted following the experimental animal care and procedure (Faculty of Veterinary Medicine, Benha University, Benha, Egypt).  The basal diet was formulated to meet the rat’s nutrient requirements in maintenance as given by National Research Council (1995) and Reeves et al., (1993).’

The current study involved a total of 70 male white albino Wister rats, weighing between 180-200 grams. The rats were procured from the Center of Laboratory Animals at the Faculty of Veterinary Medicine, Benha University, Egypt. Prior to the experiment, the rats were given a one-week period for acclimatization. Throughout the study, all rats had access to standard laboratory balanced commercial diet and water ad libitum. The research adhered to the guidelines for experimental animal care and procedures set by the Faculty of Veterinary Medicine, Benha University, Benha, Egypt. The basal diet provided to the rats was formulated based on the recommended nutrient requirements for rat maintenance as outlined by the National Research Council (1995) and Reeves et al. (1993).

please rephrase this paragraph 'The estimation of some biochemical parameters such as enzymes' activities in blood, tissues, and body fluids plays a major role in assessing drug safety. Urea and creatinine activities in plasma were determined spectrophotometrically by specific kits as mentioned above according to methods described by (Kaplan et al., 1984, Murray, 1984) respectively, following the instructions of the manufacturer.
Oxidative and antioxidative parameters in kidney homogenate were determined spectrophotometrically using specific kits according to (Ohkawa et al., 1979), (Paglia and valentine 1967), (aebi 1984) for MDA, GSH, Catalase, following the instructions of the manufacturer. Histo-pathological examination was done according to) Banchroft et al 1996).’

The assessment of various biochemical parameters, including enzyme activities in blood, tissues, and body fluids, plays a crucial role in evaluating the safety of drugs. Urea and creatinine activities in plasma were measured using specific kits and determined spectrophotometrically, as mentioned earlier, following the methods outlined by Kaplan et al. (1984) and Murray (1984) respectively. The manufacturer's instructions were followed for the assay procedures.

The oxidative and antioxidative parameters in kidney homogenate, such as MDA, GSH, and Catalase, were also determined spectrophotometrically using specific kits. The methods described by Ohkawa et al. (1979), Paglia and Valentine (1967), and Aebi (1984) were followed for the respective assays. The manufacturer's instructions were adhered to during the analysis.

For the histo-pathological examination, the protocols outlined by Banchroft et al. (1996) were followed.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Statistical analysis was performed by using GraphPad Prism 8.0.2 software. Numerical data in this study were expressed as mean±standard deviation (M±SD) and categorical data as percentages. Analysis of variance (ANOVA) and a post hoc Tukey test were used. The difference between groups was defined to be statistically significant when a p value ≤0.05.’

GraphPad Prism 8.0.2 software was employed for the statistical analysis of the data. The numerical data are presented as mean ± standard deviation (M ± SD), while categorical data are expressed as percentages. Analysis of variance (ANOVA) followed by a post hoc Tukey test were used for comparisons. A p-value ≤0.05 was considered statistically significant to determine differences between groups.

please rephrase this paragraph 'This study was carried out in strict accordance with the recommendations in the Guide for the Care and Use of Laboratory Animals of the National Institutes of Health 8th edition and adhered to principles published by International Council for Laboratory Animal Science (ICLAS) and those of Benha University Animal Care and Use Committee under number of (03-03-23).’

Conducting this study followed rigorous adherence to the guidelines provided in the 8th edition of the Guide for the Care and Use of Laboratory Animals from the National Institutes of Health, as well as the principles established by the International Council for Laboratory Animal Science (ICLAS). The study was also approved by the Benha University Animal Care and Use Committee under number (03-03-23).

please rephrase this paragraph 'Concerning biochemical parameters results, rats administrated Acrylamide showed significant (p<0.05) elevation of creatinine and urea levels in serum when compared to the control group. Considerable improvement in these parameters was observed following cinnamon and / or rosemary oil administration and these results were recorded in Table (1) Regarding the results of oxidative stress (Figure 1&2&3) the rats administrated acrylamide showed a marked rise in MDA level and significant decline in CAT and GSH in renal tissues compared with control groups. Considerable improvement in these parameters was observed following cinnamon and/or rosemary administration. Histopathological examination of renal tissues in acrylamide treated group revealed alterations in their histological architecture. An improvement in histopathology was observed following cinnamon and / or rosemary administration (Figure 4).’

Regarding the results of biochemical parameters, rats administered Acrylamide exhibited a significant (p<0.05) increase in serum creatinine and urea levels compared to the control group. However, notable improvement in these parameters was observed following administration of cinnamon and/or rosemary oil, as indicated in Table (1). 

In terms of oxidative stress results (Figure 1&2&3), rats treated with acrylamide displayed a considerable elevation in MDA levels and a significant reduction in CAT and GSH levels in renal tissues compared to the control group. However, significant improvement in these parameters was observed following cinnamon and/or rosemary administration. 

Additionally, histopathological examination of renal tissues in the acrylamide-treated group revealed alterations in their histological architecture. Nevertheless, an improvement in histopathology was observed following administration of cinnamon and/or rosemary, as depicted in Figure 4.

please rephrase this paragraph 'Data are presented as (Mean ± S.E).  S.E = Standard error.
Mean values with different superscript letters in the same column are significantly different at (P<0.05).’

The data is presented as (Mean ± S.E), with S.E representing the standard error. Mean values with distinct superscript letters within the same column indicate a statistically significant difference at (P<0.05).

please rephrase this paragraph 'Acrylamide group showed (B&C) Focal degeneration and desquamation were detected in the lining epithelium of some tubules with atrophy of individual glomerular tufts at the cortex.
Cinnamon group showed (D) no histopathological alteration. Rosemary group showed (E) no histopathological alteration. ACR+Cin group showed (F) There was no histopathological alteration as recorded. ACR+ Ros group showed (G) There was no histopathological alteration as recorded.
ACR+Cin+Ros group showed (H) There was no histopathological alteration as recorded.’

In the Acrylamide group, focal degeneration and desquamation were observed in the lining epithelium of certain tubules, along with atrophy of individual glomerular tufts in the cortex (B&C).

In the Cinnamon group, no histopathological alterations were detected (D). Similarly, no histopathological alterations were observed in the Rosemary group (E). 

For the ACR+Cin group, no histopathological alterations were recorded (F). Likewise, in the ACR+Ros group, no histopathological alterations were observed (G). 

The ACR+Cin+Ros group also exhibited no histopathological alterations (H) as documented in the study.
